mechanism_narrative: Declining payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 131043.0 thousand as of May 2011
regime_hint: post-crisis recovery with fragile labor market
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or negative 20-day forward returns do not occur in > 55% of instances where PAYEMS declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate remains near zero (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%) while inflation (CPIAUCSL) begins accelerating, signaling monetary policy constraint that will eventually force tightening, triggering equity repricing downward over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.09% and CPIAUCSL at 224.804 as of May 2011
regime_hint: zero lower bound with emerging inflation pressures
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction term (FEDFUNDS × CPIAUCSL momentum) shows Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) signal strengthening residential investment and consumer confidence, which drive aggregate demand and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 523.0 thousand units as of April 2011
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in < 50% of instances where HOUST rises > 5% month-over-month, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates below its 3-month trend while unemployment (UNRATE) remains elevated above 8.5%, signaling supply-side weakness and persistent slack that constrains profit margins and equity valuations downward over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 92.9815 and UNRATE at 9.1% as of May 2011
regime_hint: weak recovery with persistent labor slack
failure_condition: Negative 5-day forward returns occur in < 52% of triggered instances, or the joint interaction (INDPRO deceleration × UNRATE elevation) shows Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) decline month-over-month, signaling weakening consumer demand that reduces near-term corporate revenue expectations and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 387117.0 million dollars as of May 2011
regime_hint: fragile demand environment with consumer uncertainty
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in < 53% of instances where RSAFS declines > 1% month-over-month, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window while payroll employment (PAYEMS) contracts, signaling accelerating labor market deterioration that triggers equity risk-off positioning and negative forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months AND PAYEMS month-over-month change < 0
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 9.1% and PAYEMS at 131043.0 thousand as of May 2011
regime_hint: labor market deterioration phase in recovery cycle
failure_condition: Negative 60-day forward returns occur in < 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction term (UNRATE rise × PAYEMS decline) shows Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real short-term interest rates (approximated by FEDFUNDS minus CPIAUCSL inflation momentum) turn negative and remain negative, signaling monetary accommodation that supports equity valuations through lower discount rates, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.09% implies deeply negative real rates
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary accommodation regime
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in < 50% of observations where real rates are negative, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 2% annualized while housing starts (HOUST) fall below 500 thousand units, signaling demand-side weakness that reduces earnings growth expectations and equity valuations downward over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth < 2% AND HOUST < 500 thousand units
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13441.9 billion (Q1 2011) and HOUST at 523.0 thousand as of April 2011
failure_condition: Negative 60-day forward returns occur in < 54% of triggered instances, or the interaction term (low GDP growth × low housing starts) shows Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.
